fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Fixing x:A, we must construct an identification from H \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} to \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen H x\right \rparen \mathclose {} . By the naturality () of H at the identification H x\colon f x = x, we have an identification H \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \cdot \mathsf {act}_{1_{A}} \mathopen {}\left \lparen H x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen H x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \cdot H x.

Simplifying, we have H \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \cdot H x = \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen H x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \cdot H x; canceling H x on either side, we therefore have the desired identification H \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen H x\right \rparen \mathclose {} .

fr:title.text: Naturality of homotopies onto the identity

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Naturality of homotopies onto the identity

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let H \colon f \sim 1_{A} be a homotopy from f\colon A \to A onto the identity function. Then we may construct a higher homotopy from H\circ f to \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ H.

Fixing x:A, we must construct an identification from H \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} to \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen H x\right \rparen \mathclose {} . By the naturality () of H at the identification H x\colon f x = x, we have an identification H \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \cdot \mathsf {act}_{1_{A}} \mathopen {}\left \lparen H x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen H x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \cdot H x.

Simplifying, we have H \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \cdot H x = \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen H x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \cdot H x; canceling H x on either side, we therefore have the desired identification H \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen H x\right \rparen \mathclose {} .
